Implement streaming response handler for real-time data processing #142

Open opened 3 days ago by developer123

This pull request adds support for streaming responses in the API layer. The implementation uses server-sent events to deliver real-time updates to connected clients.

Changes

  • Added StreamHandler class for managing concurrent connections
  • Implemented backpressure mechanism to prevent memory overflow
  • Added comprehensive test coverage for edge cases
  • Updated documentation with streaming examples

Testing

All existing tests pass. Added fifteen new unit tests and three integration tests covering the streaming functionality. 

npm run test -- --coverage
All 89 tests passing
Coverage: 94.2%
reviewer1 commented 2 days ago

Great work on this implementation. The backpressure mechanism looks solid. A few comments below on the error handling approach.

reviewer1 left a review comment

Consider using a more explicit error recovery strategy here. The current approach might silently swallow connection errors that the caller needs to know about for proper cleanup.

developer123 commented 1 day ago

Thanks for the feedback. I have updated the error handling to propagate connection errors through the event emitter pattern. This allows callers to register error handlers while maintaining backward compatibility.

The updated implementation also includes automatic retry logic with exponential backoff for transient network failures.

reviewer2 commented 12 hours ago

Looks good to me. The retry logic is a nice addition. One minor suggestion would be to make the maximum retry count configurable through the options object rather than hardcoding it.

ci-bot commented 6 hours ago
Build passed Tests passed (89/89) Coverage: 94.2%

reviewer1 - Approved
reviewer2 - Commented
